description: Instruction-output pairs to train an AI to generate professional, visually rich PowerPoint presentations using pptxgenjs. Each sample includes a user prompt, structured slide plan, and annotated pptxgenjs code.

samples.user_prompt: Create a professional 5-slide quarterly business review presentation for Q3 2024 with KPIs, revenue chart, and action items.
design_principles_used: Dark title + dark closing slide (sandwich structure)
Card grid with shadow for KPIs
Top accent bar on cards for color identity
Native bar chart with styled axes
Two-column layout with color-coded header bars
Numbered action items with icon circles

samples.user_prompt: Build a 6-slide startup pitch deck for a SaaS company raising Series A. Include problem, solution, traction, team, and ask slides.
design_principles_used: Large charSpacing for premium brand feel on title
Highlighted middle stat card for emphasis
Pillar cards with colored top bands
KPI rectangles with contrasting text
Avatar initials with colored oval for team slide
Use-of-funds breakdown with accent lines

samples.user_prompt: Make a 5-slide training presentation on 'Effective Communication in the Workplace' for onboarding new employees.
design_principles_used: Full-height sidebar as brand element on cover
Numbered agenda with alternating color squares and divider lines
Icon+circle cards for four equal concepts
Color-coded do/don't columns with light background tints
CTA button using filled rectangle with centered text

samples.user_prompt: Create a 5-slide annual marketing performance report with channel breakdown, funnel, and recommendations.
design_principles_used: Highlighted center card in scorecard row for emphasis
Side-by-side pie + bar for dual-view channel analysis
Styled native table with alternating row fill and colored header row
Two-line recommendation items (bold title + muted body)
Dark closing slide with brand red accent squares

samples.user_prompt: Build a 4-slide product roadmap presentation showing Q1–Q4 milestones, feature priorities, and a vision slide.
design_principles_used: Vertical timeline bands per quarter with milestone chips
2x2 priority matrix with top accent bars per quadrant
Split-panel layout for closing slide (ask vs Q&A)
Coordinated ocean blue palette across all slides
Rounded rectangles for feature pill cards in matrix

samples.user_prompt: Create a 4-slide HR presentation showing employee engagement survey results with scores, highlights, and action plan.
design_principles_used: Top header bar as brand band on cover
Vertical fill bars as custom gauge/bar for scores
Color encoding: green=strong, amber=neutral, red=at-risk
Quote cards with open-quote icon in brand color
Dark action cards with emoji icon, color top bar, and owner attribution

samples.user_prompt: Create a 4-slide competitive analysis presentation covering market positioning, feature comparison table, SWOT, and strategic takeaways.
design_principles_used: Split cover panel with agenda bullets on light side
Comparison table with emoji checkmarks and color coding
SWOT 2x2 quadrant grid with per-quadrant color coding
Numbered takeaway cards in 2x2 grid on dark background
Consistent charcoal minimal palette throughout

samples.user_prompt: Create a 4-slide budget proposal for a new department initiative, with cost breakdown, ROI projection, and approval request.
design_principles_used: Header band with metadata row for branded cover
Key-value row list with divider lines for executive summary
Doughnut chart paired with formatted cost table
Two-series line chart showing cost vs benefit crossover
Dark approval slide with metric cards and named approvers

slide_types.title_dark: Dark background, large heading, subtitle, light footer text. No accent line under title — use whitespace or background color instead.
slide_types.kpi_cards: Row of equally-sized white cards on light background. Each card: colored top accent bar (0.07" tall), metric label (small, muted), large number (brand primary), delta vs prior period (green/red).
slide_types.bar_chart: Native pptxgenjs chart. Always style: custom chartColors, clean chartArea fill, muted axis labels, subtle valGridLine, no catGridLine, showValue with matching dataLabelColor.
slide_types.line_chart: Native pptxgenjs chart. lineSmooth: true, lineSize: 3. Same axis/grid styling as bar_chart.
slide_types.two_column: Two equal-width cards side by side. Each has colored top bar matching its theme (green=positive, red=negative). Header in matching color.
slide_types.icon_row: 4 cards, each with oval icon circle at top, bold title, description text below. Cards same width, same height.
slide_types.quote_card: White card with colored top bar, large open-quote glyph, italic body text, tag label in brand color at bottom.
slide_types.swot_2x2: 4 quadrant boxes in 2x2 grid. Each has colored top bar, bold colored header, bullet list items. Color code: strengths=dark, weaknesses=amber, opportunities=green, threats=red.
slide_types.numbered_dark: Dark background, numbered items using squares or circles with brand color fill, white number text, item title + description.
slide_types.comparison_table: pptxgenjs addTable with colored header row, alternating row fills, emoji checkmarks (✅/❌) color-coded green/red.
slide_types.timeline: Quarterly columns, each with colored header rectangle, vertical center bar (light gray), milestone chip shapes per item.
slide_types.funnel_table: Simple addTable with stage names and conversion rates, colored header, alternating rows.
slide_types.split_cover: Left panel: dark fill, large title. Right panel: white background, agenda bullets with colored square markers.

anti_patterns: Never use '#' before hex color values — causes file corruption
Never use accent lines under titles — hallmark of low-quality AI output
Never add colored header/footer bars just for decoration
Never default to cream/beige backgrounds (F5F5DC, FAF0E6, etc.) — use white or brand palette
Never encode opacity in 8-char hex colors — use the opacity property
Never reuse option objects across multiple addShape/addText calls — PptxGenJS mutates them
Never use unicode bullets (•) — use bullet: true in text options
Never use negative shadow offsets — corrupts file; use angle: 270 for upward shadows
Never use ROUNDED_RECTANGLE with rectangular accent overlays — corners won't align
Never stack all text in text-only slides — always add at least one visual element

color_application.rule: One dominant color (60-70% visual weight), 1-2 supporting tones, one sharp accent
color_application.dark_light_sandwich: Dark title slide + dark closing slide, light content slides in between
color_application.card_accent_bar: Top 0.06-0.08" colored bar on white cards provides color identity without overwhelming
color_application.shadow_values: Standard card shadow: type=outer, color=000000, blur=6-8, offset=2, angle=135, opacity=0.08-0.12

typography.title: 36-44pt bold, brand primary or white
typography.caption: 10-12pt, muted gray (#6B7280 or #9CA3AF), often italic
typography.charSpacing_for_labels: Use charSpacing: 4-8 on uppercase sub-labels/eyebrows for premium feel

spacing.between_cards: 0.15-0.2 inches gap between cards in a row
spacing.title_to_content: 0.3-0.4 inches below title before content starts

fine_tuning_instructions.input_format: User natural language prompt describing presentation topic, audience, and number of slides
fine_tuning_instructions.output_format: Valid pptxgenjs Node.js code that creates a complete, visually professional .pptx file
fine_tuning_instructions.training_objective: Model should learn to: (1) select an appropriate color palette for the topic, (2) choose varied layouts per slide, (3) produce syntactically correct pptxgenjs code, (4) apply all anti-patterns as hard constraints
evaluation_criteria: Does the code run without errors?
Are all hex colors 6 chars with no # prefix?
Does each slide have at least one non-text visual element (shape, chart, or image)?
Are layouts varied across slides (no two slides identical structure)?
Is the color palette consistent and topic-appropriate?
Does the closing/title slide use a dark background?
Are there no accent underlines under titles?
